  <subtitle>Pattern defines animation sequences, queueing logic, common variants, and boundary conditions for slot collect mechanics</subtitle>
  <facts>
    <fact>Collection sequence includes CASH flying (top→bottom, left→right), dimming collected symbols, JP conversion to numbers, and JP flying animations</fact>
    <fact>Multiple COLLECT symbols queue and process sequentially from top to bottom</fact>
    <fact>Collection order priority is CASH first, then JP after number conversion</fact>
    <fact>Action order for interactions is Bomb → Multiplier → Collect</fact>
    <fact>GRAND/MEGA jackpots bypass normal collection flow and trigger full-screen award panels</fact>
    <fact>JP symbols restore to text labels only after all COLLECT processing completes</fact>
    <fact>Pattern documents 11 common variants including single vs multiple COLLECT, upgraded Collect (Boost/Multi/Chain), Multiplier integration, Bomb integration, CASH splitting, CASH upgrading, mystery events, 5-tier JP, bidirectional Collect, Burst injection, and per-reel collectors</fact>
    <fact>Burst variant handles edge case where COLLECT exists on reels 1+6 but no CASH on reels 2-5 by injecting guaranteed CASH</fact>
    <fact>Upgraded Collect types (Boost/Multi/Chain) have defined action order with Chain symbols receiving Boost/Multi bonuses</fact>
  </facts>
  <narrative>The collect-feature pattern provides comprehensive specification for slot game collection mechanics. The base pattern defines a sequential animation flow: CASH symbols fly to COLLECT positions using FlyManager/tween with spine animations (CashFly), collected symbols dim immediately, JP symbols convert to numbers via 0.3s tween with NumberAnimation, then fly to COLLECT. Multiple COLLECT symbols queue and process top-to-bottom. The pattern documents 11 variants including advanced mechanics (Boost/Multi/Chain upgrades requiring accumulated COLLECT counts), interaction patterns (Multiplier and Bomb integration with defined precedence), visual upgrades (bronze→silver→gold based on thresholds), mystery event integration (reel 5 respins adding more COLLECT), bidirectional collection (Eye Strike2's opposite-direction flows with FlyManager direction parameters), Burst injection (Wrath of Thunder v2's guaranteed-win mechanism), and per-reel collectors (Super FG's 3-symbol collection creating reel-specific COLLECT). Edge cases cover critical sequencing: CASH before JP, GRAND/MEGA independent panels, COLLECT queueing, Bomb→Multiplier→Collect precedence, JP label restoration timing, Burst vs normal collect priority, and upgrade action ordering. This pattern serves as canonical reference for implementing collection mechanics across UK slot game projects.</narrative>
